sources.label: Morningstar - After a 160% AI-fueled rally, can Caterpillar stock keep climbing?
Caterpillar IR - AIP/Boyd CAT 2 GW hyperscale AI power alliance (dated Jan 28, 2026; not new)
TradingKey - CAT mover note, Jun 9, 2026 intraday weakness
StockAnalysis - CAT analyst price targets (avg ~$912 vs ~$902 spot)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since Jun 6; the headline AI-power catalysts (AIP/Boyd CAT 2 GW Monarch order, Kenosha eFRAME capacity add) predate the window and are fully in the price. Yesterday's verdict already moved it down for the post-rerating crowding; today's -1.5% drift is not a setup change, so hold with mild negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Genset/turbine backlog for AI data centers keeps compounding (six 1GW+ public contracts, ProPower 2.1 GW, AIP 2 GW), and Energy & Transportation is now the growth engine management guides to 5-7% sales growth through 2030.
verdicts.neutral: A $416B megacap up ~51% YTD trading at consensus average target (~$912-937 vs $902) with Zacks Strong Buy and universal sell-side coverage; AI power demand is real but offers near-zero hidden-alpha on a passives/bottleneck list that rewards under-owned names.
verdicts.bear: The AI-power narrative is the most consensus trade in industrials; any hyperscaler capex digestion or data-center genset order air-pocket would compress a multiple that has already rerated 160% year-over-year, and core construction/mining segments remain cyclical ballast.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings / new gigawatt-scale data-center power orders or Power segment guidance raise.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding unwind: post-rerating multiple compression if hyperscaler capex or genset order momentum decelerates.
